reasoning: To resolve YES, Mauritania would need over 10 times its baseline average of 'Strategic developments', meaning ~24 or more events recorded by ACLED in just 30 days (Feb 6 – Mar 8, 2026). 'Strategic developments' usually encompass arrests, troop movements, looted infrastructure, etc. Such a massive volume of events would only occur during a massive political crisis, coup d'état, or the sudden outbreak of nationwide conflict. A comprehensive search of Jan/Feb 2026 news for Mauritania reveals no such events — no elections, no major crackdowns, and no new conflict fronts. In fact, standard news involves economic development and stability. Mauritania remains remarkably resilient to the violence ravaging the Sahel (Mali, Burkina Faso), and without any signs of a crisis, it is near-impossible that the number of strategic developments will spike to 10+ times normal. I assign the lowest allowable probability (0.02).
